explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text segments referencing financial/legal risk, illicit substances, or terrorism/subversion** (such as credit ratings/downgrades, narcotics investigations, drug abuse warnings, or legal statutes on secession/terrorism).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears in multilingual news and official reports (English, Spanish, Italian, Hebrew, Slovak, etc.) covering: financial instability (credit ratings, refunds, credit cards), law enforcement and legal contexts (drug busts, warrants, arrests), public health warnings about drugs, and national security legislation targeting terrorism/subversion.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts describe routine, neutral, or positive events (TV shows, weddings, scientific discovery, personal news, general announcements) with no mention of financial risk, illicit activity, or legal/security threats. The high-activation group consistently contains tokens related to institutional risk or illegality.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects formal or reportive language concerning societal and institutional dangers—primarily financial risk, controlled substances, and political/security threats. It acts as a broad "risk and illegality" filter across diverse linguistic and topical contexts.

results.actual_text: la fecha, existen por lo menos 300 solicitudes al programa crediticio que insistió, no termina con el año, sino que será abi
results.plain_text: Por Alfredo Guevara  Una línea de crédito de hasta 250 mil pesos, con una tasa de interés del siete al nueve por ciento a pagar en cinco años con garantía y aval, ofreció el Gobierno del Estado a través del financiamiento “Tam-emprende”, para todo tipo de negocios que quieran invertir, para el proyecto de remodelación de la alameda 17.  El Subsecretario de Pequeña y Medianas Empresas Jesús Villarreal Cantú confirmó que algunos comerciantes de la Capital del Estado, como de otros municipios, de una u otra forma se han acercado a la dependencia con el objeto de informarse de los requisitos para acceder a ese tipo de financiamiento que no termina en este año.  Reveló que a la fecha, existen por lo menos 300 solicitudes al programa crediticio que insistió, no termina con el año, sino que será abierto por nacional financiera de desarrollo, a efecto de que los interesados estén en condiciones de informarse y presentar los requisitos para acceder a los empréstitos.  “Hay un impulso de Nacional Financiera por 130 millones de pesos el cual en 15 días se agotó, cuando generalmente se lleva en un año, pero la realidad es que la gente quiere crédito, algo que no estaba sucediendo de ahí que el programa Tam – emprende, represente una de esas opciones para el financiamiento de proyectos” señaló.  Villarreal Cantú explicó que si bien se establece la tasa de interés del siete al nueve por ciento, también existe otro del 13 o 14 por ciento para acceder a un empréstito de hasta cinco millones de pesos, dependiendo del proyecto a desarrollar.  Al crédito, estableció que pueden acceder los comerciantes que estén interesados en un proyecto a desarrollar con motivo de la modernización de la alameda o calle 17 en esta Capital, donde podrán ofrecer sus productos según corresponda al giro a desarrollar.  Si bien ese tipo de financiamiento venía fuerte para el área de desarrollo rural, el funcionario explicó que por la firma de un convenio del Gobernador Francisco García Cabeza de Vaca estará abierto a toda clase de negocios.  “Se han estado acercando de Victoria como del resto del Estado” indicó.

predictions.text: Villarreal Cantú explicó que si bien se establece la tasa de interés del siete al nueve por ciento, también existe otro del 13 o 14 por ciento para acceder a un empréstito de hasta cinco millones de pesos, dependiendo del proyecto a desarrollar.

results.actual_text: of the Department of Justice report, which describes a pattern of civil rights abuses by narcotics detectives. The report, based on a two-year investigation, documented multiple instances where
results.plain_text: SPRINGFIELD — The Springfield chapter of the NAACP is calling for Mayor Domenic Sarno to remove Police Commissioner Cheryl C. Clapprood, saying she lacks the ability to turn around a department put on the spot by a scathing U.S. Department of Justice report documenting recurring civil rights violations. In a statement Wednesday, Bishop Talbert W. Swan II, the chapter president, wrote: “The NAACP does not have confidence that Commissioner Clapprood can implement the reforms required to ensure accountability and change needed to direct the Springfield Police Department.” Swan said Clapprood lacks the leadership skills needed to make systemic changes within the department. Sarno, in response, said he has full confidence in Clapprood and that she is not going anywhere. “I support Commissioner Clapprood 110% and she will not be fired,” he said in a statement. All of this comes in wake of increased criticism of the police after the release in July of the Department of Justice report, which describes a pattern of civil rights abuses by narcotics detectives. The report, based on a two-year investigation, documented multiple instances where narcotics detectives used excessive force by slapping, punching and kicking suspects during arrests. It also found instances where officers falsified reports to conceal the use of force and injuries, and said supervisors routinely looked the other way. The report covers a period prior to Clapprood becoming police commissioner roughly 18 months ago. Clapprood was selected as interim commissioner in February 2019 following the abrupt retirement of John Barbieri. She was appointed permanent commissioner 12 months ago, and is under contract until May 2024. Swan said in an interview with The Republican that, even though she was not in charge during the period reviewed by the Justice Department, Clapprood was a member of the department’s senior leadership, first as a captain and then as deputy chief, during that time. “She is completely ingrained in the system and the corruption. How is she going to be the one to implement change?” Swan said. “She did not come into that position and start to clean house,” he said. Sarno responded that the department under Clapprood has made “significant changes” to address the Justice Department report, and more are coming. At a City Hall press briefing last week, examples of change cited by Sarno and Clapprood included the revamping of Narcotics Unit procedures and personnel, requiring officers and detectives to wear body cameras while on the street, and creating a website for people to file complaints. Clapprood called reform “a work in progress.” Swan also criticized Sarno for declining to take part in an Oct. 4 digital town hall sponsored by the NAACP and the Pioneer Valley Project. Sarno’s refusal to attend, as he declined to participate in a similar event in June, shows the mayor’s attempt “to control the narrative” by controlling the microphone, Swan said. The mayor’s unwillingness to go before the public except on his own terms does “little to engender trust with the public whose rights have been violated,” Swan said. Sarno, Clapprood, retired Supreme Judicial Court Chief Justice Roderick Ireland — tapped by Sarno to assist with reforming the department — and several other city officials are scheduled to take part in an online forum on police issues Saturday at 10:30 a.m. Swan was invited to a similar event in June but declined, Sarno said. The mayor said Swan is invited to participate in the Saturday event if he chooses.

predictions.reasoning: Direct mention of narcotics detectives and excessive force, matching illicit substances and legal risk.

results.actual_text: feeling and something we haven’t experienced for all those years, and it’s a credit to all those young ones that have come on-board.” Mr Cross said Fletcher International
results.plain_text: The prospect of rolling up their sleeves and putting in some hard work is not deterring Albany youth, according to the city’s biggest employer. In light of alarming recent youth unemployment figures for WA, Fletcher International general manager Greg Cross said he believed Albany’s youth were bucking a growing negative perception over the work ethic of young jobseekers. The Narrikup abattoir employs close to 500 people, with most of the workforce made up of youth aged 17-24. According to a recent report by the anti-poverty group the Brotherhood of St Laurence, the Wheatbelt region, which includes Albany, has a youth unemployment rate of 15.9 per cent, ranked 13th in the 20 worst regions for youth unemployment nationwide. North-west Perth, which includes Joondalup, Yanchep, Wanneroo and Scarborough, is the worst in WA at 16.7 per cent. Mr Cross said he had seen an increased local flavour to the workforce in the past two years, showing youth were prepared to roll up their sleeves even though there were parts of the job which were “not so savoury”. He said about 95 per cent of the work was like working in a butcher’s shop and it was not all “blood and guts”. “There has certainly been many occasions over our 21 years when we have had to look to recruiting interstate or overseas and that has naturally been what has happened to this industry on a national basis,” he said. “However, what we have experienced here in Albany in the last two years is the demand for labour we have needed has been accommodated by all locals, therefore we haven’t had to rely on backpackers or certainly haven’t had to seek any kind of overseas workers. “It’s a nice feeling and something we haven’t experienced for all those years, and it’s a credit to all those young ones that have come on-board.” Mr Cross said Fletcher International was also a registered training organisation and new workers could complete a 12-week traineeship and bring home between $800 and $900 a week. “Everything you normally read about or hear about the youth of today and that they don’t want to work, well in Albany we should tip our hat to the young ones here,” he said. ATC Worksmart chief executive Peter Adams said it was important young jobseekers were fully committed to wanting to work as there were generally limited opportunities in Albany. “I believe it has become harder as many small business in our region have struggled to remain viable and their obvious first response is to lower labour costs, thus reducing employment opportunities,” he said. Sign up for our emails

results.actual_text: Jeremy Corbyn ready to fight Labour suspension in court after antisemitism report Jeremy Corbyn is
results.plain_text: Jeremy Corbyn ready to fight Labour suspension in court after antisemitism report Jeremy Corbyn is preparing to launch potential legal action if his suspension from the Labour party is not lifted. The former leader was suspended yesterday after insisting that the extent of antisemitism in the party had been “dramatically overstated for political reasons”. He has made clear that he will challenge the decision. “Unless Keir [Starmer] is prepared to do a deal I think the only route is legal,” a key ally of Mr Corbyn told The Times today, citing the “strength of the EHRC’s views on political interference” in disciplinary cases. “I do not believe Keir was not involved in the decision,” the ally said. If his membership is not reinstated, Mr Corbyn could potentially take Labour to court and argue that it is not

results.plain_text: NANJING, China, May 6, 2021 /PRNewswire/ -- Suning Group, today announced that it signed a framework agreement for the establishment of a New Retail Development Fund with Jiangsu Province State-owned Assets and Nanjing Municipal State-owned Assets. The New Retail Development Fund is jointly funded by Suning, State-owned Assets and Nanjing Municipal State-owned Assets and social capital, with a total size of RMB 20 billion.  As a local enterprise rooted in Jiangsu Province, Suning is always focusing on its main retail business, and actively integrating into the new development pattern of "dual circulation". The fund will support Suning in adjusting and optimizing its structure, revitalize high-quality assets, achieve transformational development, and promote the high-quality development of the new retail industry.  Based on market principles and the rule of law, the fund will adopt methods such as investing in Suning's high-quality assets and high-quality businesses under the premise of safeguarding investment risks. It will further utilize the leading role of Suning as a modern business circulation enterprise to realize win-win cooperation between state-owned and private resources, and to promote the sustained and rapid development of the modern commerce circulation system.  About Suning Group  Founded in 1990, Suning Group has two listed subsidiaries at home and abroad and more than 300,000 employees around the world, ranking the third in the Chinese private sector. Being committed to its mission of Leading the Industrial Advancements in Creating a Higher Quality of Life for All, it provides quality products and services to more than 700 million customers worldwide and has its business scope covering three main sectors: Retail, Real Estate, and Financial Service. Suning.com, the main subsidiary is a leading O2O smart retail service provider in China. In 2020, Suning.com maintained its position among the Fortune Global 500 and topped the 2020 List of China's 500 Most Valuable Brands with a brand value of RMB 296.815 billion.  SOURCE Suning Group
